seoTitle: Pegs Creek vs Pelican Point — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Pegs Creek and Pelican Point. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Pegs Creek vs Pelican Point: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Pegs Creek and Pelican Point.
heading: Pegs Creek vs Pelican Point — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Pegs Creek, Western Australia and Pelican Point, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Pegs Creek has a lower overall climate risk score (31/100) compared to Pelican Point (33/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Pegs Creek or Pelican Point safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Pegs Creek has a flood risk score of 44/100, while Pelican Point scores 32/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Pegs Creek scores 25/100 for bushfire risk. Pelican Point scores 48/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Pegs Creek or Pelican Point?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Pegs Creek has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.5% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
